title: smoky velvety ethiopian turkey ham tibs
description: peppery and velvety ethiopian dish featuring turkey ham, great for a weekend brunch. try serving with a side of injera for a more authentic experience.
instructions: heat 2 tablespoons of niter kibbeh in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
add the thinly sliced turkey ham and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
remove the turkey ham from the skillet and set aside.
add the remaining 2 tablespoons of niter kibbeh to the skillet, then add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
add the berbere spice, tumeric, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
add the cooked turkey ham back into the skillet and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro and accompanied by flatbread or injera if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
